steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to CONTRAST the two tile styles. A successful answer must put both styles side-by-side and highlight how they differ, drawing on the defining detail from the notes: majolica designs are painted directly on the tile, while arista designs are stamped into the tile.

steps.title: Find the choice that delivers the contrast
steps.content: Only the sentence with "whereas" states both methods and sets them against each other. The others either name both styles without distinguishing them or describe just one.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Tiles in the majolica and arista styles can be found in the Royal Alcázar of Seville in Andalucía, Spain.: tells where the tiles are located; no contrast of styles. ✗
  • Choice B — Featuring tiles in the majolica and arista styles, the Royal Alcázar of Seville in Spain is famous for its intricate tilework.: mentions both styles but only describes the palace's fame, not how the styles differ. ✗
  • Choice C — In the arista style, designs are stamped into the ceramic tiles, whereas in the majolica style, the designs are painted directly on them.: explicitly contrasts the two defining techniques. ✓
  • Choice D — Among the famous tilework of the Royal Alcázar of Seville are majolica style tiles, made by painting designs directly on the ceramic tiles.: describes only majolica; no contrast with arista. ✗
C is correct.
